mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces discount rates for equity valuations, directly increasing present value of future corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0 percent signals severe labor market slack and depressed consumer spending, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives higher corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a directional accuracy > 55% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery regime reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which supports short-term equity momentum and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum yields negative 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of observations during the specified regime, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level above 218.0 indicates accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations to produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the inflation threshold, or out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month payroll employment growth signals strengthening labor demand and rising household income, which sustains corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during payroll growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of positive real GDP growth and a federal funds rate below 0.5 percent creates an expansionary monetary environment that amplifies aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns with a directional accuracy > 55% over a 3-year rolling window, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion regime signals capacity constraints and manufacturing slowdown, which reduces earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO momentum yields positive 60-day SPX returns in > 55% of observations during the specified regime, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.
